topic: Mag-gravity coupling constant 1.67 (verification re-review after ISS-2155/ISS-2157 patches)
our_argument_summary: WIN-012 retains Self-Contradicted verdict: kappa = 1.67 nT/uGal is derived as a ratio of BOU magnetometer numerator over Mohe 1997 gravimeter denominator, but WIN-013/014 report 0.0 uGal for the denominator term, leaving kappa undefined. The argument is unchanged from c7; this run verifies that ISS-2155 (TLDR factual error fix) and ISS-2157 (WIN-070 cross-reference) landed cleanly without introducing new defects.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No live calculation. Live-power tile compares 'observed' 1.677 (which equals 10.9/6.5, recomputing the same hardcoded ratio that defined kappa) against 'predicted' 1.67 with 5% tolerance. Status is hardcoded PASS.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Fully hardcoded. The 'check' is mathematical identity on the same two numbers that defined kappa.
data_sources_used: BOU magnetometer (INTERMAGNET; numerator date ambiguous per ISS-2156)
Mohe spring gravimeter, Wang et al. 2000 (denominator, unreplicated)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Tolerance 5% on a constant defined to equal itself.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new in c8. The dome's internal BOU-event-date ambiguity (2003 G5 Halloween storm in WIN-012 Para 1 vs 2017 eclipse in WIN-012 Para 4 and live-power tile) remains an open dome-internal inconsistency, tracked as ISS-2156.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c7. Live-power Domain EM-Gravity Coupling tile: Predicted 1.67, Observed 1.677, Tolerance 5%, Status PASS. The 'observed' value 1.677 = 10.9/6.5, recomputing the same hardcoded ratio that defined kappa. No live fetch; no comparison against independent measurement.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c7. The dome relabels (i) the Chapman Sq-dynamo ionospheric current explanation of eclipse magnetic anomalies and (ii) the thermal-tilt-artifact / shielding interpretations of the Mohe gravity signal as a unified 'magnetic-gravity coupling.' No new physical mechanism is derived.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c7. kappa = 1.67 was computed post-hoc from two pre-existing published observations (Mohe 1997; BOU 2003 Halloween storm OR BOU 2017 eclipse depending on dome page — see ISS-2156). Both observations predate the kappa definition. No pre-registration.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c7. kappa is a ratio of two independently measured field values; it cannot be derived from disc_radius, firmament_height, or any dome-geometric parameter.

patches_in_scope.details: tldr_evidence rewritten from the c7-flagged factually-wrong text ('a disputed gravity reading by a magnetic measurement — both from the same unreplicated 1997 experiment') to text that correctly identifies BOU (Boulder, Colorado) and Mohe (China) as different sites, instruments, and events. The patched TLDR matches the recommended fix from c7 Hole [0] modulo the addition of '(BOU magnetometer)' as parenthetical clarification — that addition is a strict improvement (it specifies the BOU instrument). Length grew 288 -> 412 chars, consistent with the longer accurate framing.

WIN-070 cross-reference appended verbatim to the closing Cross-references paragraph. Cross-checked r=0.992 and AI=1.152 against WIN-070.finding — both numbers match WIN-070's own characterization ('r = 0.992 and AI = 1.152 are static HTML'). detail_evidence length grew 4339 -> 4516 chars, consistent with one appended sentence.

patch_verification.new_defects_introduced: None. The TLDR rewrite deliberately omits a BOU event date (the dome-internal date ambiguity is still ISS-2156-tracked); sidestepping that question in the TLDR is acceptable because the body carries the (still-flawed-internally) detail. The WIN-070 cross-ref does not duplicate any existing reference and appears in the natural cross-references list position.
patch_verification.counter_arg_considered: A dome defender could argue the new TLDR is rhetorically weaker: by admitting 'two different sites, different instruments, different events' it gives the appearance that we're attacking a methodological-diversity issue rather than a self-contradiction. But the tldr_verdict immediately closes this — 'If the signal is zero, the coupling constant is undefined' is the structural argument, and it does not depend on what type of events were measured. The diversity admission is honest and does not weaken the verdict; it removes a factually-false framing that a defender could otherwise have used to discredit the entire review entry. Net: the new TLDR is stronger, not weaker.

unresolved_prior_cycle.status_check: remains open, assigned-analyst per open-issues.json — WIN-012/Part 6.14 cross-page BOU-event-date inconsistency. Decider routed correctly: this hole needs analyst analysis (not a sed-patch) because it requires deciding canonical attribution and surfacing the dome's own internal contradiction.

remains open in open-issues.json — bundles c7 minor holes H4/H5/H6 (inline kappa arithmetic, Yang & Wang 2002 citation, PRED-TIER annotation). All marked low-priority by c7 recommendations; no urgency. Decider may safely leave open or roll into a future EXP.

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider's patch package this run was well-targeted. The two patches addressed the highest-severity c7 hole (Major TLDR factual error, ISS-2155) and a structural rebuttal-locator gap (Moderate cross-reference, ISS-2157) — exactly the two holes a sed-style patch could close cleanly without requiring fresh analytical work. The two carry-over issues that were NOT applied this run (ISS-2156 internal-inconsistency, ISS-2158 minor-bundle) are correctly routed to analyst (for the former — it needs a decision on canonical attribution) or held as low-priority (for the latter). The patches are textual fixes that preserve the existing argument structure (data/wins.json:WIN-012.tldr_evidence and data/wins.json:WIN-012.detail_evidence).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: This is verification scope: the patches resolve specific c7 holes; they do not change the WIN-012 verdict, which remains Self-Contradicted (data/wins.json:WIN-012.verdict). The underlying self-contradiction (WIN-012 claims 6.5 uGal eclipse gravity anomaly to define kappa, WIN-013/014 claim 0.0 uGal; kappa = X/0 is undefined) is not the kind of issue a patch resolves — it is structural and is what drives the verdict.

deeper_analysis: The verification confirms both ISS-2155 and ISS-2157 patches landed correctly with no new defects. Two carry-over open issues from c7 remain (ISS-2156 moderate, ISS-2158 minor-bundle). Per PROP-038, this WIN-012 item correctly stayed in curmudgeon (Opus) scope rather than handing off to curmudgeon-verify (Sonnet) — gate (b) failed because the c7 review carried 6 holes total (1 major + 2 moderate + 3 minor), exceeding the <=2-and-all-minor threshold. The Opus pass adds value here because (a) it cross-checks WIN-070's r/AI numbers against the new WIN-012 cross-ref, (b) it considers the counter-argument that the rewritten TLDR is rhetorically weaker (answered: no, the verdict tldr handles the structural argument), and (c) it confirms ISS-2156 is correctly routed to analyst rather than sed-patched. PROP-038 gate logic working as designed: this verification properly required Opus.

citations_verified: WIN-070 r=0.992 and AI=1.152 — match WIN-070.finding verbatim
citations_unchecked: DOIs in carry-over body (Unnikrishnan 2001 PRD 63 062002, Van Camp 2001 10.1023/A:1013940928637) — already verified in earlier cycles; no new citations introduced this round.

summary_for_decider: Verification PASSED for queue_id 392 (WIN-012, class=verification). Both ISS-2155 (TLDR factual error) and ISS-2157 (WIN-070 cross-ref) patches landed cleanly. No new holes. Verdict (Self-Contradicted) unchanged. Two c7 carry-overs remain open in open-issues.json: ISS-2156 (moderate, BOU 2003 vs 2017 internal inconsistency — correctly routed to analyst as 'assigned-analyst') and ISS-2158 (minor-bundle, low-priority) — both already tracked, no action needed from this review. Recommend: pop queue_id 392, no patch this run. Note for tinker: per PROP-038 the next iteration of WIN-012 verification (if c8 holes_found stays at 0) may be eligible to hand off to curmudgeon-verify (Sonnet); this run didn't qualify because c7 had >2 holes. Side observation: ISS-2154 appears in BOTH open-issues.json (status:open) and closed-issues.json (status:fixed) — that's a data inconsistency the decider should reconcile on next run (not curmudgeon-scope; flagging because I crossed it while validating the SEC-6.13 skip rationale per PROP-038 gates).
